text: Excuse me sir, sir, is this your McLaren? Yeah, both is mine. How did you get rich, man? I go all over the world interviewing billionaires and successful people. What did you do for a living? I'd rather not say. I'm from the States. I've interviewed 32 billionaires in the last four years. Right. I came out here to interview successful Hong Kong entrepreneurs. Right. What types of businesses did you work? I have a few. What made you want to become an entrepreneur? My family was really poor. My dad grew up really poor. He did. And he made it all himself. And he always told me that I would find my own way. So you ended up making it yourself? Yeah, I mean I never got any help from my family. So are you from Hong Kong? I am, I am born and raised. So what was the most amount of money you made in a single year? Wow, probably 100 million pounds. 100 million pounds? Yeah, yeah, yeah. How was that possible? What business was that primarily? We do global markets. We do IPOs and everything. You take companies public? Yeah, yeah, yeah. You actually? Yeah, yeah, yeah. How did you get into that business? Sorry, sorry, sorry. Sometimes you got to do business mid-interview. One more. One more. What's up? How old are you now? I'm 31. There's a young 20-year-old watching this right now. Right. They want to make 100 million dollars, 100 million pounds in today's world. Give them the blueprint right here. Wow. You know, everyone has their own way out. There's no way you can copy me. There's no way I can copy you. So everyone's different, right? I understand that from competition. It's competitive. No, no, I mean, my market's really niche. We only have a few people in the business. So the riches are in the niches? Definitely. But did you come upon that niche of anything you could have done? I mean, I have a double degree in economics and law. What's important? Book smarts or street smart? Street. Definitely street. I don't care where you graduated from. If you're only book smart, then you might be working for me anyway, right? And that's why they say, see students from the world. Yeah, yeah, yeah. I mean, I never ask anyone who works for me. I never require them to have any specific educational wise. How did you get your street smart? I really don't know how to tell you, but just like you and I, you're street smart. You guys are all street smart, I can tell. It's born, right? It's born with it. Is it inside you? It's definitely born with it. You cannot pretend for it. It doesn't work that way. What's the name of that car right there? Pagani. How much did that cost? 2.5 million pounds. And you bought it cash? Yeah, both of them. You see a lot of young people, they make a little bit of money and then they go buy a car. Was it like that for you in the beginning? No, no, no. Why not? I'm a really traditional Chinese person. I want to bring back to the family, I want to pay back to the family, and then I will spend it on myself. Thank you so much for your time, man. Thank you. What was your name, man? Bobby. Bobby, means a lot to me. I actually saw you guys on Instagram before. I'll follow you guys.
